title: Long-video stage-gap scales with frames (aimdo VRAM<->RAM offload on 32GB) — separate from the MPI-191 volume tax
description: Live-found 2026-07-05 (5090 32GB, MPI-194 hot-store SHIPPED + 42GB transformer confirmed on container disk, warm gens): the LTX stage-1->stage-2 gap SCALES WITH VIDEO LENGTH and the hot-store does NOT fix the long-video case. Warm gaps measured: short ~9s, 5s video 45s, 10s video 111s. Smoking gun on the 10s gen: VRAM usage DROPPED to 17/32GB while RAM ROSE to 66/92GB (short videos ran ~29-31GB VRAM, low RAM). => aimdo OFFLOADS the working set VRAM->RAM because the 10s latent set + 42GB transformer won't co-fit in 32GB VRAM; the stage boundary then pays a heavy VRAM<->RAM shuffle + transformer re-fault that GROWS with frame count (a fixed 42GB disk re-read would cap ~45s flat regardless of length — 111s at 10s proves an additional frame-scaling cost). NOTE stage-2 also includes 'getting ready for inference' setup. DISTINCT root cause from MPI-191 (network-volume read bandwidth, correctly fixed by MPI-194 disk staging — do NOT reopen that). User also running a 30s video to probe for OOM (2026-07-05). NEXT (user-directed): (1) reproduce LOCALLY on a 32GB+ card — does the frame-scaling gap appear off-pod? isolates aimdo-inherent vs pod-specific; (2) reproduce on the OFFICIAL RunPod comfyui template (stock stack, our LTX_t2v.json, 5s+10s) — does stock aimdo do the same? (3) pull the pod log at the 10s (and 30s) stage-1->2 boundary to classify the gap: model reload vs VAE-decode vs offload churn vs OOM. Levers to weigh AFTER diagnosis: keep-transformer-resident, bigger-VRAM host floor (minMemoryInGb / larger card), --reserve-vram tuning, tiled/chunked VAE decode, or accept long videos are offload-bound on 32GB. Read docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md tier model (page-cache->disk->volume) before testing. ||| STOCK-TEMPLATE REPRO DONE 2026-07-05 (runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0, 5090 32GB, 62GB RAM, models on container disk, same LTX_t2v workflow + pinned nodes): warm 5s gap 12s (full 114s), warm 10s gap 22s (full 149s) vs OURS 45s/111s. VERDICT: frame-scaling gap is OUR STACK, not aimdo-inherent-on-32GB (stock does same job with LESS RAM). Mechanism: stock holds 42GB in system RAM post-gen (VRAM 748MiB idle) -> stage-2 loads VRAM<-RAM; ours re-reads 42GB from disk each stage (aimdo pinned pool defeats page cache, MPI-191). Lever = RAM-residency parity: diff launch flags/aimdo version+config/wrapper memory mgmt vs stock. Env capture from stock pod pending. ||| STOCK-POD BISECT ROUND 2 (2026-07-05, same stock pod, one flip at a time, warm 10s gap each): ComfyUI v0.27.0 @ bb131be9 (our exact pin) = 21s ACQUITTED; + --preview-method taesd = 19s ACQUITTED; + all 7 node packs at our node_lock pins (VHS/Impact/Subpack/Frame-Interp/USDU added, Impact needed grep -v sam2 requirements + ultralytics due to template pip constraint) = 19s ACQUITTED. Stock pod now mirrors every software axis flippable there and stays ~19s vs our 111s. REMAINING SUSPECTS: (a) our image base (nvidia/cuda:13.0.3-runtime-ubuntu24.04 python/libs), (b) wrapper spawn (stdout PIPE, input/output/extra-paths flags), (c) host RAM shape — stock pod 62GB vs our pods 92GB; aimdo pool sizing may scale with RAM (ours offloads VRAM 17/32 mid-gen, stock holds 42GB pinned RAM post-gen and does not offload). NEXT: manual pod from cubric-vision-pod:v0.12.0-cu130 with env CUBRIC_COMFY_ENABLED=0 + expose 8188, dl.sh models to /root/ltx-models, launch /opt/ComfyUI/main.py BARE stock-style, warm 10s: ~19s = wrapper guilty (bisect wrapper extras), ~111s = image base guilty. Record host RAM at create. ||| ROUND 3 (2026-07-05, manual pod from OUR image v0.12.0-cu130, 5090, 125GB RAM, EUR-IS-1): bare stock-style ComfyUI launch on port 8189 (CUBRIC_COMFY_ENABLED=0 clobbered by start.sh line 245 export=1 — side-launch workaround; models re-DL to /root after Edit-Pod container recreate wiped /root) = warm 10s gap 19s. IMAGE ACQUITTED, HOST/RAM-SIZE ACQUITTED. Remaining: wrapper path vs WORKFLOW FILE — user insight: all pod-side tests so far ran the AUTHORING TEMPLATE (LTX_i2v_t2v_template), while the app runs the generated API-format file (comfy_workflows/LTX_t2v.json + runtime injection). Now A/B-ing the generated file on the same bare 8189 instance. Side findings to card separately: (a) start.sh hardcodes export CUBRIC_COMFY_ENABLED=1 — pod-env override impossible, should be ${VAR:-1}; (b) RunPod Edit-Pod RECREATES container (wipes /root) — 'restart survives' only true for in-place restarts. ||| ROUND 4 FINAL (2026-07-05, same our-image pod, replay of the EXACT app payload captured from LOCAL comfy /history, 3 lora backslashes healed): bare 8189 warm total 82.6s; through wrapper (/wrapper/prompt, Bearer) warm 87.8s @512x960 — WRAPPER ACQUITTED; full-res 704x1216 10s warm through wrapper 121.6s (disk) and 118.3s (small models on volume) — RESOLUTION and VOLUMExRES ACQUITTED warm (cold volume = 205.7s, known cold tax). Cache gotcha: identical seed re-run = 0.2s full graph cache hit — always bump seed (node 77) when replaying. EVERY pod-side axis now acquitted. REMAINING SUSPECT: the app-driven flow — preview/event relay (WS backpressure over internet) or app-side event lag inflating the PERCEIVED gap; the original VRAM-17/32+RAM-66 observation was on an app-created pod config, so real churn there not fully excluded. NEXT SESSION: real app gen on app-created pod WITH .expose-comfy debug door; compare app-UI gap vs pod /history execution duration (timestamps in status.messages). Disagree -> event pipeline convicted; agree -> diff that pod config vs today's manual pod. Replay tooling in scratchpad: app_payload.json/replay_body*.json + heal_replay.py; wrapper endpoints: POST /wrapper/prompt, /wrapper/restart-comfy, GET /wrapper/history/{id} (Bearer CUBRIC_TOKEN). ||| ROUND 5 EPHEMERAL (2026-07-05): app-created EPHEMERAL (disk-rooted) pod, real app-driven 10s 704x1216 warm gap 31s / total 107s (/history == UI, both runs) vs 111s warm gap on volume app pods. EVENT PIPELINE ACQUITTED; VOLUME CONVICTED as gap multiplier at 10s. Hot-store-can't-fix verdict OVERTURNED for 10s: disk-resident deps = 31s in the real flow. Open: why 111s despite MPI-194 transformer staging (loader resolving volume copy? or sub-15GB deps re-read per thrash cycle). Fix path: full dep-set disk hot-store + verify staged path wins resolution. ||| ROUND 6 (2026-07-05 evening): 111s NOT REPRODUCIBLE on current runtime. App volume pod (EU-RO-1, wrapper 0.2.27, dedupe clean, hot-store staged): 10s 1216x704 warm gap 34s / cold 32s — matches ephemeral disk pod (31s). Fix stack shipped 03:30-04:46 (wrapper 0.2.24 loop fixes + MPI-193 dedupe + MPI-194 hot-store) — attribution not isolated, gap fixed. 5/5 runs UI==pod /history: app pipeline permanently acquitted. Open: length scaling re-test (20s/30s) on fixed stack + product length policy; cleanup .expose-comfy + pods. ||| ROUND 6b: 20s @1216x704 warm on fixed stack = gap 77s / total 209s — mild super-linearity (+13% over linear from 10s), old-stack blowup GONE. 30s extrapolates ~2min gap vs 620s morning. Length policy now a quality question (30s output mediocre), not perf. ||| ROUND 7 FINAL: pod log per-phase timing = app tax ZERO (boundary 48.4-48.9s constant, submitter-independent; step-1 = page-cache warming trend; UI gap = event-visibility artifact, not a metric). Remaining cost named: stage-2 loads LTXAV with 0 patches (lora-less) = different model -> 40GB re-stage mid-prompt. FIX: same lora stack in stage-2 at strength 0 (lora law: strength flips don't reload). Boundary should drop to single digits. ||| ROUND 8: lora-rewire DISPROVEN live (boundary 48.4->63.2s with stage-2 lora'd; '0 patches' reload was bookkeeping, not the cost). Stage-2 stays lora-less. Floor = legit inter-stage work + ~20s warm 40GB re-fault post-eviction = structural on 32GB/bf16. Kills: fp8 or bigger VRAM. Perf investigation COMPLETE. ||| CLOSE (user-verified): user's own app-vs-browser A/Bs = always close, app sometimes faster. Tier decision: balanced = mxfp8 (Blackwell) + fp8_scaled (Ada), both Kijai distilled-1.1; NVFP4 rejected; low tier deferred. Research: research/quant-tiers.md.
